honest-check/src/honest_check/suppressio: tree-sitter yields comment nodes in source (== line) order, so _collect_directives already builds the list in line order; sorting by line is identity. Battery-verified that walk order equals line order across nested functions, classes, if/else, blank lines, and trailing comments, so removing the sort cannot change the output.

Sorting by the rules frozenset (index -1) instead of the line (index 0): the input is already in line order (battery-verified), and frozenset '<' is the subset relation — disjoint or equal rule sets compare False both ways, so list.sort is stable and preserves the existing line order. The output is identical to sorting by line.
honest-check/src/honest_check/startup.py: Emptying the "warn" key. startup_check dispatches via _ON_ERROR.get(on_error, _on_warn), whose default IS _on_warn, so on_error='warn' resolves to _on_warn whether the key is present or absent. Behaviour is identical.

Emptying the on_error default ('warn' -> ''). A caller who omits on_error then gets ''; _ON_ERROR.get('', _on_warn) falls to the default _on_warn — exactly what 'warn' resolves to. Behaviour is identical.

The surviving swap moves the "warn" key onto a sibling name. Verified exhaustively that only warn->sibling swaps preserve behaviour (raise->* and halt->* are all caught by the warn/raise/halt assertions): the sibling's own value wins the duplicate-key collision so raise/halt are unaffected, and get('warn') falls to the default _on_warn. Behaviour is identical.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:str: The script entry guard. __name__ is the module's dotted name under import, never '__main__' and never '', so emptying the literal leaves the guard False under conformance exactly as the real guard is. main() itself is exhaustively tested via cli_main.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:rem: The `if __name__ == '__main__'` block runs only when cli.py is executed as a script (python -m / direct), never under import-based conformance. main() is exhaustively tested via cli_main, so the one-line entry wrapper is unreachable from the suite by construction.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:sol: Body of the script entry guard, reached only when cli.py is run as a script — never under import-based conformance. main() is tested directly via cli_main.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:rem: Diagnostic is used only in the local variable annotation `diagnostics: list[Diagnostic] = []` inside _run_once. Python does not evaluate local variable annotations at runtime (PEP 526), so removing the import cannot raise — verified that a local annotation referencing an undefined name does not error.

_find_config falls off the end of its ancestor loop returning None when no honest-check.toml is found, so the explicit `return None` is redundant — verified the function returns None with the line removed.
